title: 10 best herbal tinctures for shortness of breath
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for shortness of breath are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants that have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. These tinctures work by soothing and relaxing the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion, and improving oxygen flow to the lungs. Examples include Lobelia (Lobelia inflata) and Thyme (Thymus vulgaris), which have a calming effect on the respiratory system, and Elecampane (Inula helenium) and Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis), which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the bronchial tubes. By using these tinctures, individuals suffering from shortness of breath can expect improved oxygen intake, reduced wheezing, and increased overall quality of life, allowing them to participate in daily activities with greater ease and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The tincture's active ingredients, such as echinacea and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier for oxygen to pass through and alleviating respiratory distress. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further reducing symptoms of shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower contains antioxidant compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.

Expectorant properties: Kansas coneflower contains mucilages that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it an effective natural expectorant for relieving respiratory congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids in Echinacea angustifolia may help alleviate shortness of breath by acting as anti-inflammatory agents and potentially reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds, which include echinacoside, may contribute to the reduction of shortness of breath by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which could help soothe the respiratory system.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as kaempferol and quercetin, may help alleviate shortness of breath by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which could help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Leaves: They are used for their ability to stimulate the respiratory system and promote expectoration, helping to relieve congestion and shortness of breath.
Stems: They are used for their reported ability to reduce inflammation and improve air quality in the lungs, thus alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in the fall season.
Chop 1 to 2 pounds of the harvested roots into small pieces for increased surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 to 100 proof vodka in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its potent extract, Adiantum majus, has a profound impact on respiratory health. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and relax the airways, reducing constriction and obstruction caused by allergies or chronic conditions. Additionally, maidenhair tree's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, making it easier to breathe and promoting healthy lung function. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and COPD, offering relief from shortness of breath and improving overall respiratory well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which may contribute to improved breathing.

Vasodilation and blood circulation enhancement: Ginkgo's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation may help increase oxygen delivery to the body, alleviating shortness of breath caused by reduced lung function or cardiovascular issues.

Bronchodilatory effects: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo extracts can relax airway muscles, leading to improved airflow and reduced bronchial constriction, which may help alleviate respiratory symptoms like shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: A terpene derivative that helps improve lung function and reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.

Flavonoids (primarily quercetin and isorhapontigenin): A class of phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and improve oxygenation, thereby easing shortness of breath.

Ginkgotoxin: A biflavonoid that has vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow to the lungs, reducing congestion and oxygenating the body more efficiently, thus alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make tinctures as they contain ginkgolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and lignans, which help to improve circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or 1/2 pound of dried leaves for tincture preparation.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces to increase the surface area for solvent absorption.
Combine the chopped leaves with 80-100 proof vodka in a 1:3 leaf-to-solvent ratio in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks or 2-4 weeks for a stronger tincture.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its natural menthol content relaxes the airways, allowing for easier breathing. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce constriction in the bronchial tubes, opening up pathways for smooth airflow. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the respiratory system help to alleviate feelings of anxiety and tension that can exacerbate shortness of breath. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal peppermint tinctures provide natural relief from shortness of breath, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath, and help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles in the bronchial tubes, reducing spasms that can constrict the airways and make breathing difficult.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a terpene that helps alleviate shortness of breath by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce airway inflammation and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Volatile oils (limonene, linalool, and beta-pinene): These terpenes have bronchodilatory effects, which help relax airway muscles and increase lung capacity, alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are used due to their high menthol content, which helps to ease respiratory issues and relieve shortness of breath.
Stems: The stems of Peppermint contain menthone and limonene, which aid in reducing inflammation and improving breathing.
Buds: Peppermint buds are used due to their high concentration of essential oils, including menthone and menthyl acetate, which help to relax airways and alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at peak potency during sunny days.
Chop the harvested leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife to release oils.
Combine the chopped mentha x piperita with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight for a strong tincture.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and steep it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains thymol, a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs. This allows for easier breathing and reduces congestion, making it an effective remedy for bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the airways, further easing respiratory distress. By promoting healthy lung function, herbal thyme tinctures can provide natural relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by constricting air passages and reducing lung capacity.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat respiratory infections such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and sinusitis, which can cause shortness of breath by inflaming the lungs and airways.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe.
Rosmarinic acid: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, rosmarinic acid may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Thymus vulgaris are rich in thymol, a compound with expectorant properties that helps to relieve respiratory issues.
Flowers: The flowers of Thymus vulgaris are used to make tinctures due to their high content of thymol and other volatile oils that help to ease congestion and promote breathing.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used to make tinctures for their high concentration of thymol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from healthy plants in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the fresh leaves in a single layer on paper bags or a clean cloth.
Combine 1 part of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Label the tincture bottle with the date the tincture was made and the ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with shortness of breath because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and improve lung function. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root help to ease bronchial constriction, allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen intake. Additionally, the soothing properties of licorice tincture can calm irritated airways and mucous membranes, further alleviating shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Mucilage production: Licorice root stimulates the production of mucilage, a protective substance that soothes and lubricates the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and constriction of airways, which can help alleviate shortness of breath.

Adrenal cortex support: Licorice root has been shown to support the function of the adrenal cortex, which produces cortisol, a hormone that helps regulate breathing and response to stress. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals with chronic respiratory conditions who may experience exacerbations due to stress.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures help alleviate shortness of breath by reducing inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid in the tincture helps alleviate shortness of breath by its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which open up the airways and improve breathing.

Flavonoids (specifically Isoliquiritigenin): Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures, such as isoliquiritigenin, help alleviate shortness of breath by their antioxidant properties, which protect the lungs from oxidative damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and opens up airways.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their bioactive compounds that help in relieving bronchial spasms and improving respiratory function.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their flavonoid and saponin content, which helps in reducing inflammation and improving breathing in individuals with respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a 10% moisture content and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and dry them at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture.
Combine 50 grams of dried root pieces with 750 milliliters of 70% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant and expectorant properties. When taken internally, the eucalyptus oil helps to clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, further alleviating shortness of breath symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and respiratory distress.
Antispasmodic property: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can cause breathing difficulties.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a terpene, helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe.
Cineole: Cineole, also a terpene, acts as a bronchodilator, allowing for increased airflow and reducing congestion.
Caffeic Acid: Caffeic Acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate the underlying causes of shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound responsible for their decongestant and expectorant properties.

Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making them effective in relieving shortness of breath.
Stems: The stems of the plant also contain eucalyptol and other compounds that help in relieving congestion and promoting easier breathing, making them a viable option for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Clean the leaves and stems thoroughly to remove dirt and debris using a fine mesh strainer.
Combine the cleaned eucalyptus globulus plant material with 50% ethanol by volume in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it is a natural bronchodilator, meaning it can help relax and expand airways in the lungs. The antioxidant properties in sage tincture also reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making breathing easier. Additionally, sage's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, further relieving shortness of breath symptoms. By addressing these underlying issues, sage tinctures can provide natural relief for individuals experiencing shortness of breath, promoting improved lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties.

Bronchodilatory effects: The volatile oils present in sage, particularly camphor and thujone, have been shown to dilate bronchial airways, making it easier to breathe and helping alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps with shortness of breath by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may reduce inflammation in the airways and improve respiratory function.
Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties that may help alleviate shortness of breath by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs and airways.

Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that may help with shortness of breath by inducing relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress, which can contribute to improved breathing patterns and respiratory function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are commonly used for tinctures as they contain high amounts of essential oils, including thujone, which has been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues.
Flowers: Salvia officinalis flowers are used for their calming and expectorant properties, helping to reduce shortness of breath by loosening mucus and promoting easier breathing.

Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which is used in tinctures to alleviate respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath, by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried salvia officinalis with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the dark for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with shortness of breath because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the airways. The mucilage in mullein helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By reducing inflammation and clearing out mucus, mullein tinctures can provide significant relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Mullein helps relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can exacerbate shortness of breath.
Mucolytic properties: Mullein's saponins break down mucus, making it easier to expel and clearing the airways, which can help alleviate shortness of breath caused by congestion or excess mucus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's flavonoids and phenolic acids reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissues, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with shortness of breath by relaxing the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing inflammation, and increasing oxygen flow.
Verbascoside: This phenylethanoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the airway muscles, thereby alleviating shortness of breath.
Leptospermone: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, meaning it helps to widen the airways and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Stems: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease breathing difficulties.
Flowers: Employed for their demulcent properties, providing a soothing effect on the mucous membranes and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried plant material in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure highest potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for tincture extraction.
Combine 1 part of plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to ease respiratory issues. The bitter principles in yarrow, such as achillein and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the airways, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and bronchial tubes, further alleviating shortness of breath. As a result, yarrow tinctures can provide relief for individuals suffering from chronic bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions characterized by shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the airways, which can constrict breathing passages and exacerbate shortness of breath.
Expectorant: Yarrow's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that may be contributing to shortness of breath.

Vasodilator: Yarrow's vasodilating effects can help dilate blood vessels, improving oxygenation of the body and reducing strain on the respiratory system, which can help alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating shortness of breath.

Flavonoids (specifically, apigenin and luteolin): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the lungs and airways from damage and reduce inflammation that contributes to shortness of breath.

Essential oil components (specifically, borneol and camphor): These volatile compounds have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties which may help alleviate respiratory issues.
Flowers: Used for their expectorant properties which can help loosen and remove mucus from the lungs.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties which may help calm the respiratory system and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:2 part ratio of fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the early morning when dew is still present.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently shaking off any dirt or debris from the fresh achillea millefolium.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned achillea millefolium with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it gently every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of hyssopus officinalis flowers and 2 parts of leaves in the morning or after the rain.
Dry the plant material for 1-2 weeks in a warm and dry place to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Combine the dried plant material with 60% ethanol or vodka in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Store the mixture in a glass container with a lid for 2-6 weeks in a cool and dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its calming properties help to relax the airways, allowing for easier breathing. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems from a trusted source or cultivate your own plants.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 70% to 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a dark cupboard, shaking the jar daily, until the liquid has turned a deep green color.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool place for at least 2 weeks before using.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for tincture production.
Prepare the plant material by gently crushing or grinding it into small pieces.
Combine the prepared plant material with the solvent in a single container.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and allow it to steep for several weeks.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages. The tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and debris, making it easier for the body to breathe. Additionally, pot marigold has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate breathing difficulties and ease congestion.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
Expectorant properties: The saponins in pot marigold tinctures help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion-related breathing difficulties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that can help to relax bronchial muscles, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with shortness of breath.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain oil-rich saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing respiratory issues such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at the peak of bloom and clean them thoroughly removing any debris.
Combine one cup of the cleaned calendula flowers with three cups of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep undisturbed.
After the steeping period has passed strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle filled to the top and store it in the refrigerator using within six months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with shortness of breath because its mucilages soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can help to open up airways, allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen intake. Additionally, slippery elm's demulcent properties create a protective barrier that coats the respiratory tract, shielding it from further irritation and promoting healthy mucus production, which can help to clear out excess phlegm and promote smoother airflow.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath by opening up airways and reducing bronchial constriction.

Demulcent properties: The mucilage in slippery elm forms a protective barrier that soothes and lubricates the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, making it easier for people with shortness of breath to breathe more comfortably.

Expectorant properties: Slippery elm's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, which can be particularly helpful for people who experience chronic bronchitis, asthma, or COPD-related shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with shortness of breath.
Bark: The bark of the slippery elm plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems in the early morning or late evening for maximum potency.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by rinsing it gently under cold running water and removing any debris.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and pack them tightly into a clean glass jar leaving about one inch of space at the top.
Combine 50% vodka or other high-proof spirits with 25% distilled water in a separate container and slowly pour it over the packed plant material to extract the medicinal properties.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the mixture daily to allow the tincture to mature and stabilize.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with shortness of breath because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax and soothe the respiratory system. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers at peak bloom and dry them immediately.
Measure out 20 grams of dried flowers and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka over the flowers and seal the jar tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, at room temperature, for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the plant for optimal potency.

Chop the cleaned and dried marrubium vulgare into small pieces to increase its surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped herb with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged in the liquid.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate even extraction and maceration.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean the marsha flowers ensuring they are free from debris and contaminants.
Combine one part dried marshmallow root with one part vodka in a clean glass jar.

Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and use within one year.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality viburnum opulus leaves and stems, ensuring freshness and free from contaminants.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 8-12 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a coarse powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to enhance surface area.
Steep 1 part viburnum opulus powder in 2 parts high-proof vodka (at least 80% abv) for 6 weeks, shaking daily, allowing extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing tincture in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried inula helenium roots at dawn or dusk for optimal potency and quality.
Combine one part dried root material with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal tinctures are asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and bronchitis. Herbal remedies such as thyme, eucalyptus, and mullein can help soothe and relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. Tinctures containing herbs like ginkgo biloba and passionflower may also improve lung function and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and panic attacks, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with shortness of breath is a blend of Ashwagandha, Elecampane, and Thyme. Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety, while Elecampane supports lung health and relieves congestion. Thyme, rich in expectorant properties, helps to break down mucus and promote easy breathing. When combined, these tinctures can provide a powerful and natural remedy for shortness of breath, promoting relaxation and improved respiratory function. This combination can be taken in small doses, ideally three times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal tinctures are beneficial for shortness of breath associated with pulmonary emphysema due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The study, which systematically reviewed 22 articles on the role of plant-derived secondary metabolites in managing emphysema, found that certain herbs such as Scutellaria baicalensis Georgi and Monascus adlay, as well as phytochemicals like gallic acid and quercetin, can improve lung function and alleviate symptoms. These natural compounds can effectively protect the lung against damage and improve or protect the lung against this disease, making herbal tinctures a potential remedy for shortness of breath caused by emphysema.
